pattern: Whether a position is good only depends on comparing it against two fixed offsets, so a single linear scan with bounds checks settles every element independently.
hints: For each index i, the only neighbors that matter are at i-k and i+k -- work out which of those two indices actually exist before comparing anything.
An element with no existing neighbor on either side (both i-k and i+k out of range) is automatically good, since there is nothing to fail against.
Sum nums[i] whenever it is strictly greater than every neighbor among {nums[i-k], nums[i+k]} that actually exists.

edge_inputs.description: k at its maximum for this length (floor(n/2))
every element equal, nothing is strictly greater
an element good only because one of its two neighbor indices is out of range
strictly increasing then strictly decreasing, tests both neighbor directions
minimum value 1 everywhere except one peak

stress.description: random array and k within bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(100, size))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, max(1, n // 2))
    return [nums, k]
